architecture.summary: ToolDec is a constrained decoding algorithm that guides LLM token generation using finite-state machines (FSMs) constructed from tool syntax schemas. At each decoding step, the FSM maintains a current state and permits only tokens that comply with the tool's syntax constraints. The model samples from a masked probability distribution where invalid tokens are zeroed out and probabilities are renormalized. Multiple FSMs are linked via a trie structure to guide tool selection and multi-step reasoning. A separate LLM-based pipeline compresses tool documentation by removing syntax constraints, which the FSM now enforces implicitly during decoding.

biblio.title: Don't Fine-Tune, Decode: Syntax Error-Free Tool Use via Constrained Decoding

claims.statement: ToolDec improves Mistral-Instruct's accuracy in tool use from 0% to 52% on ToolEval I3-Instruction, matching the performance of ToolLLM
ToolDec eliminates all syntax errors (syntax error rate 0%) across all base models and benchmarks evaluated
ToolDec improves ToolLLM + ReAct win rate by 10 points (36.5% to 46.5%) on ToolEval I2-Category
ToolDec reduces average tokens per tool by 58% and 69% on ToolEval I2-Category and I3-Instruction respectively
ToolDec improves LLaMA-7B accuracy by 2.2x on FuncQA (6% to 13.2%) and 5.1x on KAMEL (8.2% to 42.4%)
ToolLLM + DFSDT with ToolDec achieves 50.5% win rate on ToolEval I2-Category, outperforming ChatGPT and matching GPT-4 performance

Fine-tuned specialist models like ToolLLM suffer severe performance degradation on general reasoning benchmarks (GSM8K: 1.2% vs. Llama-2-chat 23.1%), suggesting catastrophic forgetting from tool-specialized fine-tuning
Decoding-time syntax constraints via FSM are much more effective than in-prompt syntax constraints, and adding both provides negligible additional benefit over decoding constraints alone
